claim: Evidence schema, prompt, readiness contract, and tests all distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes.

evidence.summary: Evidence result schema definition explicitly declares scope field as 'project | external | user | unknown' and states 'project facts require project-scoped evidence'
evidence.why: Schema contract enforces scope distinction at the data model level

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES Set declared with exactly four values: project, external, user, unknown
evidence.why: Type constraint validates scope values are from the allowed set

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope and evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope functions normalize and validate scope declarations
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement ensures evidence records declare valid scopes

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues function returns PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE and PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blockers when project facts lack project-scoped evidence
evidence.why: Readiness contract enforces project facts require project evidence with concrete locators

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues function returns EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker when evidence records lack explicit scope
evidence.why: Blocks readiness when scope field is missing from evidence records

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness calls evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for complete evidence results
evidence.why: Readiness contract actively validates scope separation during PRD readiness checks

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt explicitly instructs agents: 'Every evidence record must include scope: project, external, user, or unknown' and 'Project facts require project-scoped evidence...with a repo-relative path or file locator'
evidence.why: Agent prompt enforces scope distinction and project evidence requirements during evidence gathering

evidence.summary: Prompt states: 'External research tools may be available; mark external records as scope=external and do not use them as project facts'
evidence.why: Prompt explicitly prevents external evidence from being used as project facts

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult validates evidence scope via evidenceScopeErrors and blocks with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED when scope is missing
evidence.why: Dispatch runtime enforces scope validation on agent results before accepting them

evidence.summary: Test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' verifies PRD readiness is blocked when only external evidence is provided for project facts
evidence.why: Test confirms external evidence cannot substitute for project evidence

evidence.summary: Test 'project evidence requires a concrete project locator' verifies PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker when project scope lacks path
evidence.why: Test enforces project evidence requires concrete file locators

evidence.summary: Test 'external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence' verifies blocker when URL is present with project scope
evidence.why: Test prevents external URLs from being mislabeled as project evidence

evidence.summary: Test 'evidence records must declare valid scope explicitly' verifies EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker when scope field is missing
evidence.why: Test enforces explicit scope declaration on all evidence records

evidence.summary: Test 'agent evidence without explicit scope is blocked during normalization' verifies agents returning evidence without scope are blocked
evidence.why: Test confirms dispatch runtime blocks agent results when scope is missing

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' verifies BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker when agents modify project files
evidence.why: Test enforces evidence gathering boundary even when agents claim success

result.notes: All four components (schema, prompt, readiness contract, tests) explicitly distinguish and enforce the four evidence scopes. Schema defines the type contract, prompts instruct agents to use it, readiness contract validates it, and tests verify all enforcement paths including external-to-project blocking, missing project locators, and scope declaration requirements.
